title: Lasiocephalus involucratus
intro: Lasiocephalus involucratus, also known as the "Yerba de San Antonio" or "Mexican Sage", is a perennial plant in the family Asteraceae, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which are beneficial for relieving congestion and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Lasiocephalus involucratus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional herbal remedies, such as cough syrups and expectorant formulas.
